description:
The state machine schema defines guards like "rework_budget_available" (schema.rs) but they're not implemented — the actual check is imperative code in lifecycle_executor.rs (max_reworks_for_phase). Move this logic into a guard evaluation system.

Changes:
1. Create a GuardRegistry trait/struct in crates/orchestrator-core/src/state_machines/ that maps guard IDs to evaluation functions
2. The "rework_budget_available" guard should check: workflow.rework_counts.get(phase_id) < self.max_reworks_for_phase(phase_id)
3. Wire the guard registry into WorkflowStateMachine so transition evaluation checks guards before allowing transitions
4. Remove the imperative rework budget check from lifecycle_executor.rs evaluate_gates logic and let the state machine handle it via the guard
5. Add tests verifying guard-based rework budget enforcement matches current behavior

This is the first step toward making the state machine the actual driver of workflow decisions rather than a mirror.

Key files:
- crates/orchestrator-core/src/state_machines/engine.rs
- crates/orchestrator-core/src/state_machines/schema.rs
- crates/orchestrator-core/src/workflow/lifecycle_executor.rs
title: Implement state machine guard registry for rework budget checks
